---
title: "Epidemiological Data Analysis using R - Introduction to Quarto"
author: 
  - Karri Seppä \newline
  - Faculty of Social Sciences, Tampere university \newline
  - Finnish Cancer Registry
institute: "<karri.seppa@cancer.fi>"
format: 
  beamer:
    aspectratio: 32
    navigation: horizontal
    header-includes:  |
      \setbeamertemplate{navigation symbols}{}
      \setbeamertemplate{footline}[page number]{}
editor: visual
---

## What Is Quarto?

Quarto is an open‑source publishing system for:

-   Presentations
-   Reports
-   Websites
-   Books

It integrates code and output.


## Why Quarto?

-   Reproducible research
-   Plain text source files
-   Multiple output formats
-   Strong RStudio integration


## Quarto vs R Markdown

-   Quarto is engine‑agnostic
-   Supports multiple languages
-   Unified format for documents & presentations
-   Future‑focused replacement


## Output Formats

A single `.qmd` file can render to:

-   HTML (Reveal.js)
-   PDF
-   Word
-   PowerPoint
-   Websites

See examples with source codes: https://quarto.org/docs/gallery/ 


## Document Structure

A Quarto file consists of:

1.  YAML header
2.  Markdown text
3.  Code chunks

Each `##` creates a new slide.

**Moodle:** Download lecture9_example.qmd, open it in RStudio and render to `html` document format.
